programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies is the 'Taskmaster' in this game show that challenges the wit and wisdom of five comedians, who are all putting their reputations on the line. Alex Horne assists Davies in setting up unique and ridiculous tasks for the competitors.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: Sarah Beeny and her family are back as they tackle more projects at their country house and farm. A muddy corner of the pond is transformed into a beautiful boathouse.

programme.title: Narrow Escapes
programme.desc: In Nottinghamshire, continuous cruiser Jay embarks on his next adventure. On the Shropshire Union Canal, Paul, Anthony and a flotilla of boaters rock up to an Elvis tribute.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, Shaun Garner and his dad Steve help Essex couple Christy and Mike find the perfect property for a permanent move to Spain. On the Costa Blanca, Darren and Natalie Brown are also on the hunt for a dream property, for Richard and Sam.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Pub landlady Alison dreams of the good life on Spain's Costa Dorada, with its golden beaches, stunning river valleys and hilltop villages. Can Jonnie Irwin find her the perfect property?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Engaged couple Sharon and Paul from Shropshire would love to own a holiday home in the seaside town of Gran Alacant on Spain's southern Costa Blanca. Jean Johansson shows them what £135k can buy.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Heather and John want a characterful bolthole on the beautiful island of Crete. Jasmine Harman shows them detached delights and an ice cream inspiration for £150k, but are any 'the one'?

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: After years of challenges, Jon and Carmen hope to finally get married. Terry and Ashley bring their vision to life. Christina and Sassan scramble to ready their guest apartment.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retail manager Jesper and tube driver David can't wait to retire and make the permanent move to the stunning countryside of Valencia. With a budget of £250,000, Jasmine Harman pulls out all the stops to find five exciting options.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First to host in East Kent is 34-year-old carpenter Wes Leaver, a dinner party virgin who has high hopes for his science-themed evening. His guests are 43-year-old estate agent Alexandra Austin, 29-year-old beautician Emma Phillips, 40-year-old ditzy dog lover Sally Parry, and 47-year-old fancy-dress enthusiast Stephen Drew.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Serbian estate agent Alexandra Austen is planning a sophisticated evening of pan-European cuisine, but the starters put Sally Parry's bean phobia under stress, while Stephen struggles to keep the goat's cheese parcel down. There's also controversial veal main, and later on tensions rise between Sally and Emma.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In East Kent its the turn of beautician Emma Phillips to host. Unfortunately the self-professed foodie finds her main described as `a pet on a plate', while straight-talking Alex describes the whole thing as rather odd. Meanwhile Stephen and Wes enjoy an impromptu beauty treatment together.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In East Kent ditsy dog-lover Sally Parry hopes to charm her guests on night four with a `Pretty in Pink' night, with posh pink food and pink table decorations. Even her pooches can't escape the pink dress code. However, the claws come out when Sally and Emma trade barbs, as Alex lets loose her inner rock chick, and weary Wesley falls asleep.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Flamboyant extrovert Stephen Drew is the last to host in East Kent. Stephen believes he can win it on hosting alone and has concocted a menu that will allow him to spend more time entertaining his guests. There's an `Ab Fab' dress code, which carpenter Wes takes to mean drag, while Sally and Alex are expecting a bit of a mardi-gras vibe, but Stephen has something a little different in mind.

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Seventeen-year-old farmer Sam seeks a tractor cab companion, preferably one that likes to dance. Can cocker spaniel Araf hit the right note?

programme.title: George Clarke's Beautiful Builds
programme.desc: In Woodford Green, widower Shaz wants to preserve her husband's memories as well as future-proof her classic Victorian terraced home for her two growing kids.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The staff at Queen's, Nottingham, are shocked as Sister Lou, one of their own, has a major bike accident. And 57-year-old Stuart's fallen off a ladder and fractured his ankle.

programme.title: Emergency
programme.desc: A mum-of-three is at St Mary's with major injuries after her taxi crashed into a lamp post. Heidi has multiple wounds from being attacked by dogs requiring major plastic surgery.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: The celebs hit the sofa for a new series. What do Rylan and his mum, Tom Allen, Mo Gilligan, Vicky Pattison, Denise van Outen, and many others, think of this week's TV?

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of the celebrities watching the most talked about TV this week are Rylan Clark Jamali Maddix, Martin Kemp, Judi Love, Zoe Ball, Deborah Meaden and Peter Jones. And Bez.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Two 19-year-olds take their first steps into the dating world together. A hip-hop artist meets another musician. And can weightlifter Jack give bodybuilder Eloise a lift?

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies is the 'Taskmaster' in this game show that challenges the wit and wisdom of five comedians, who are all putting their reputations on the line. Alex Horne assists Davies in setting up unique and ridiculous tasks for the competitors.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In south Wales, beautician Rachael and her partner Nick take a risk by cooking dishes they've never made before, and Ameel shows off his powers of memory recall.

programme.desc: In south Wales, beautician Rachael and her partner Nick take a risk by cooking dishes they've never made before, and Ameel shows off his powers of memory recall.

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The teams create 24 classic Battenberg cakes and 24 reinvented ones, before taking on a Shakespearian-inspired theatrical scene. Whose chance to progress is to be, or not to be?

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The interceptors make an early hour dash to catch a team of late night burglars and one of the suspects treats Liam to a face full of saliva. Jacko and Simon are in pursuit of a speeding driver who abandons the car and legs it.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: For the first time, a pursuit is filmed from the suspect's perspective. Plus Liam and sniffer dog Louis help uncover thousands in cash and a £1/2 million of class-A drugs. Finally, a 999 call causes firearms officers to rush to Guisborough at 11pm.

programme.title: The Last Sunset
programme.desc: A woman is pursued by three men during a Mexico-to-Texas cattle drive.

programme.title: McLintock!
programme.desc: A cattle rancher finds himself wrangling with both incoming settlers and his headstrong wife. Slapstick comedy and uproarious rows ensue when the cattleman's estranged wife turns up seeking a divorce. Based loosely on Shakespeare's `Taming of the Shrew'.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: A recycling plant needs two old portable cabins swapped out for new ones, making for some serious heavy lifting for Nick. Jim sees to an old Toyota abandoned in a live lane on the M1.

programme.title: Traffic Cops

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dog handler Jon is forced to wrestle with a drunken man, Dan has an early-morning meeting with a drink-driver and a wine-loving shoplifter gets his just deserts. Meanwhile, Mark pulls over a juggernaut in jeopardy.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A violent man gives the interceptors the runaround after wreaking havoc in a hospital, the driver of a stolen VW Beetle rewrites the highway code, a map-reading Macedonian truck driver has a bad day and a fuel thief is caught red-handed.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: Traffic chaos in the Tyne Tunnel, and shocking driving as a car reverses down a slip road with an unlicensed driver at the wheel who hasn't even taken his test.

programme.title: Air Force One
programme.desc: High-altitude thriller in which the presidential aircraft, Air Force One, is hijacked by Russian communist radicals demanding the release of the Kazakhstani leader. With the First Lady and her family trapped on board, the hijackers threaten to kill one hostage every half-hour until their demands are met. What they don't know is that the president himself is hiding in the hold and formulating a plan to save his family.

programme.desc: As bush fires threaten his local community, Mick volunteers to help others in need of supplies. Meanwhile, Sludge hauls three racers to a burn-up in the desert.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: On the North York Moors, a 50-ton construction machine and a 20-ton trailer have crashed off a narrow country lane. The rescue team at Alpha face a treacherous cross-country journey.

programme.title: They Who Dare
programme.desc: Set in the Aegean Sea and extolling the Special Boat Service. programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: In this Las Vegas Special, Officers pursue an armed suspect in a stolen car. A hit-and-run driver runs from his truck when it catches fire. An uncooperative driver hurls insults at Officers.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: In this Las Vegas Special, the Air Unit and K-9 Buddy help capture a burglary suspect. Bystanders help German tourists out of a flipped car. A disgruntled woman shatters a patrol vehicle's window.

programme.desc: Mulder and Scully visit a town under attack from killer cockroaches. When Mulder discovers they are not insects but highly complex robots, he questions whether they come from this planet at all.

programme.title: Traffic Cops

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: In North Yorkshire, more than 1,000 drink drivers are arrested every year. On one busy road, officers search for a car reportedly swerving on a dual carriageway, and set a trap.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dan Brigginshaw chases a suspect on the run, a routine vehicle check turns into a drugs bust for Suzie Ranyard, and Barry Peach has his hands full when a group of revellers clash with bouncers in Skegness.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jim is hot on the tail of a speeding motorcyclist, Jon reveals his guilty pleasure when he retrieves a stolen caravan and dog handler Barry is brought to book by a wannabe legal eagle.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: In this episode, Jim intercepts a car containing cannabis, a late-night reveller talks himself into trouble and Sergeant Baxter has his work cut out when confronted by a dishonest driver.

programme.title: Rogue Claimers
programme.desc: A globetrotter claims twice for high-end headphones lost on holiday, while an unlucky pub goer takes a cut lip all the way to court with sobering consequences.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Levi interrogates Abigail after Richie drops her home in the police car.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Mackenzie returns from visiting Tane in hospital, excited to share with Levi she's been feeling so many kicks from their baby.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Dr Razak sees Caroline, who has discovered a knotty lump on her collar bone that has got her fearing the worst. Paramedic Sophie Daley is on a home visit, checking up on Robert who is confined to his bed with Parkinson's disease.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Christine, 71, has a bleeding head following a fall. As she is on blood thinners, a scan is urgently needed. Dean has necrotising fasciitis, a flesh-eating infection.

programme.title: 999 Critical Condition
programme.desc: Cases include a builder who has had a 25-foot fall through a roof and sustained spinal fractures, a woman having an acute asthma attack, and a pregnant cancer patient undergoing surgery.

programme.desc: A woman is admitted after sustaining a nasty injury falling off a bar stool in Hull.

programme.title: The Surgeon

programme.title: Beep Beep

programme.title: Songs & Rhymes

programme.desc: Walter the Mouse and Dude the Dog need help to find an item they have misplaced.

programme.title: Songs and Rhymes

programme.title: Happy Birthday to You
programme.desc: Make your child a star on BabyTV by creating their very own personalized birthday greeting. Using a digital photo, parents can create a clip for their child which can be viewed online or submitted to BabyTV for the chance of being featured on-air.

programme.title: Bath Tubbies
programme.desc: Ducky, Ellie and Froggy are having fun fishing in the bathtub! Froggy catches something big! What can it be? A treasure chest, of course! What's inside? Come and see!

programme.title: Draco
programme.desc: Join Draco as he learns the difference between inside and out as he tries to find a place to hang up a picture of Auntie Dragoona. At first he puts it inside the cupboard, but then decides to hang it on the wall outside for everyone to enjoy.

programme.title: The World Is A Wonder
programme.desc: Come and explore the life cycle of a chicken! Join the hen with the red comb as she meets a fine rooster! After he's gone, the hen lays three eggs! She sits on them to guard them and keep them warm!

programme.title: Zeina's Detective Agency
programme.desc: A highly familiar day-to-day object arrives at Zeina's doorstep forgetting what it is used for. Searching for the answer, Zeina and her best friend Roy come up with a new and hilarious use for the surprised object.

programme.title: Welcome to the Island
programme.desc: Coco, the wise coconut tree, Bucky, the curious rock, Lily, the little flower, and Blossom, the happy papaya tree, explore themes of fostering and building friendships while having fun on their small island.

programme.title: Teeny and Tiny's Classroom
programme.desc: Teeny and Tiny are two happy and mischievous friends that share with us their riddles and games. As we follow the antics of the tiny friends, we discover shapes, colours and quantities.

programme.desc: Bunny's helmet is a little too big and doesn't fit her. She needs a smaller helmet.

programme.title: Billy Bam Bam
programme.desc: Billy and Bam-Bam venture to the playroom where they amuse themselves as any siblings do.

programme.title: Jungle Book
programme.desc: Animated series based on Rudyard Kipling's classic about the adventures of Mowgli.

programme.title: Songs & Rhymes

programme.title: The Lollipops Song Series
programme.desc: Three toddler friends who form a girl band and turn everyday ups and downs into fun.

programme.title: Hungry Henry
programme.desc: Henry wants to drink warm milk but Chef George is out of it. Join Hungry Henry on his journey to find milk. There isn't any at the grocery store or on Jack's truck, but there is milk on the dairy farm, where cows produce it! Come along, grab a jug and enjoy fresh milk with Henry!

programme.title: Mice Builders
programme.desc: Mice are devoted to building their new house.

programme.desc: Pix and Leo enjoy fun in the snow! From building a snowman to having a snowball fight, snowboarding and even ice skating, Pix and Leo have a ball in the snow!

programme.title: My Robot & I
programme.desc: Kim is a girl, Kit is her robot and together they have fun finding out about the world; Kit learns to play in the sand, paint pictures and grow plants; Kim learns new things about her robot.

programme.title: Walter & Dude

programme.title: Mowgli - Welcome to the Jungle

programme.sub-title: The Train Police: Tracking the Sex Offenders
programme.desc: Reports of sexual assault and harassment on trains and at stations are on the rise, but victims say CCTV cameras are often broken or of such poor quality that their attackers can't be identified. We follow British Transport Police officers and staff as they race against time to gather the crucial evidence they need to bring offenders to justice.

programme.title: Stormborn
programme.desc: A single Arctic fox scurries across the mountains. He's on a mission to reunite with his mate from last year, but has she survived the harsh Icelandic winter? All he can do is wait. In Shetland a mother otter has successfully raised her twin cubs, one male, one female, to the brink of independence but their journey will be far from easy, a dog otter is hanging around and determined to cause trouble.

programme.title: That Was the Team That Was
programme.desc: Scotland's 1974 World Cup team was arguably the best group of Scottish footballers ever assembled and were considered genuine contenders to win the tournament. This is the inside story of that unforgettable summer of football told by the players who represented Scotland in West Germany.

programme.title: Kevin Bridges: In Search of the Beautiful Game
programme.desc: Kevin Bridges embarks on a journey to see if the beautiful game still exists. He meets Brazil football icon Cafu, Scottish legend John McGinn, São Paulo ultras, US football fans and many others.

programme.desc: All the reaction to the World Cup draw as Scotland find out their opponents. Guests include Ryan Christie, Scott Brown and James McFadden.

programme.title: Scotland v Denmark: Through the Eyes of the Fans

programme.title: Shrek 2
programme.desc: Ogre Shrek and his new wife Fiona are surprised to discover a letter from her parents, inviting them over for dinner. Fiona is eager to show off her new husband, but her parents don't yet know she's an ogre. Taking Donkey along, they set off for Far Far Away to visit them, but the trip is hampered by the jealousy of a wicked fairy godmother and her hired assassin, Puss in Boots.

programme.desc: A mystery unravels in the jungle when a game show contestant is stabbed on a zip line. Meanwhile, the team butt heads with Mervin.

programme.desc: Five years ago, a schoolgirl was murdered by her boyfriend. It seemed like case closed, but not according to Pip Fitz-Amobi. She decides to uncover the truth and find the real killer.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Five years ago, a schoolgirl was murdered by her boyfriend. It seemed like case closed, but not according to Pip Fitz-Amobi. She decides to uncover the truth and find the real killer.

programme.title: Planet Sex With Cara Delevingne
programme.desc: Since men are having more fulfilling sex than women, Cara wants to find out why. She sets out to fix the so-called `orgasm gap'. In Germany she uncovers the science behind orgasms, makes `vagina art' in Japan and dives into a masturbation class in New York and a women-only sex party in LA.

programme.title: Planet Sex With Cara Delevingne
programme.desc: Cara grew up feeling ashamed of her sexuality. Now she wants to be out, loud and proud. In Germany, she meets queer refugees rebuilding their lives with cabaret, attends her first ever Pride in Tokyo and, in the US, parties at the biggest lesbian festival in the world.

programme.title: Love in the Flesh
programme.desc: It's day two in the beach house where our five couples are navigating their way through a real-life relationship. After a meeting for the first time in the flesh yesterday, these relationships are either on the way up or looking unlikely to last the week. The five couples go on their first dates with no screens or filters to hide behind. Zara announces that there are two private suites and that it's up to the group to decide who should spend the night in them and why.

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: Fresh new talent aim to impress famous faces and follow in the footsteps of some of stand-up's biggest names. Across a number of rounds, comedians compete to show they could be the next breakout comedy star.

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: Stay-at-home-daughter Chi is suddenly forced to fend for herself when her parents die in a car crash. Chi learns she will only inherit the house on condition she completes a list of 'Things You Should Have Done' left in the parents' will, the first of which is, ironically, learn to drive. Meanwhile, Chi's highly strung auntie Karen wants nothing to do with her dependent pest of a niece, but very much wants the house she feels is rightfully hers.

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: Chi spectacularly fails a trial period at the pharmacy with Karen, but Dave kindly lets her shadow him on his delivery rounds. On a drop-off to a retirement home Chi finds her tribe... old people. She also falls in love with Linda, an elderly lady who seems to think Chi is her husband. Elsewhere, Dave has a health scare, and Karen starts to think she might be the fertility issue.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris and Frances are in Leicester to help Hector and Claudia transform their blank new-build plot into a Cypriot cooking garden for a growing family.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's Belgian journey takes him back to Brussels to examine a dark chapter in the country's past: the brutal seizure of the Congo under King Leopold II.

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: Preserving the legacy of St Cuthbert and a colony of breeding seabirds, on the remote Farne Islands. And in Wales, experts race to save a fragile bible that shaped Welsh culture.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: Nick Robinson has a conversation with, not an interrogation of, the people who shape our political thinking about what shaped theirs.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Four more contenders face the black chair under the spotlight and the ticking clock in an attempt to become the next Mastermind champion.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: The team travel to north London to help out a family of five. In one day, they must transform the living room from an overrun play area into a space fit for all the family. Whilst upstairs they take on the challenge of creating a multifunctional home office and guest room.

programme.title: Earth's Seasonal Secrets
programme.desc: We celebrate the glorious nature of Summer on planet Earth, and the extraordinary ways animals and plants rise to the challenges it brings. With the sun shining and the flowers blooming, this is the season of splendid abundance, and the long hours of daylight make life burst out in a riot of activity. But you have to find clever ways to get your share of the good times while they last and as temperatures soar, everything has to deal with sweltering heat.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: This show from Uppingham School in Rutland features a collection of gemstones exchanged with a monk for a tin of corned beef and a kimono worn by George Formby.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The show comes from Reading in Berkshire. Anita falls in love with a couple of American dolls and Nick finds some 19th-century French paperweights which get pulses racing at the auction.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Nikki Fox, Ruthie Henshall, Danny John-Jules and Ignacio Lopez take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the final leg of his Belgian journey, Michael Portillo travels to Namur, capital of Wallonia, to discover a citadel dating back to the 10th century.

programme.sub-title: 1st Test, Day 2: England v New Zealand
programme.desc: Day 2 highlights from the Test match between England and New Zealand.

programme.title: Gardeners' World

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: Caring for the curious collections at two of the National's Trust's most unusual houses, from a boy's suit of samurai armour to stuffed birds and a living collection of rare sheep.

programme.title: Inside No. 9
programme.desc: Two police officers always have time to get to know each other on the night shift. But PC Thompson's previous partner died in a brutal attack less than a month ago, the forensics have gone missing, and PC Varney is beginning to ask a lot of awkward questions.

programme.sub-title: The One With the Ride Along

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: After Gary confides in Monica that he intends to ask Phoebe to move in with him, Monica immediately breaks her promise to him and tells Phoebe, who worries that it is too early in the relationship. programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rachel's New Dress

programme.sub-title: The One with All the Wedding Dresses
programme.desc: Distraught over Ross's engagement, Rachel suggests to Joshua that he marry her, even though they've only been on four dates. programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel and Ross reminisce separately about when they dated each other, and the friends get invitations to Ross's wedding, but not all of them are going.

programme.title: The Angry Birds Movie
programme.desc: Flightless birds lead a mostly happy existence, except for Red, who just can't get past the daily annoyances of life. His temperament leads him to anger management class, where he meets fellow misfits Chuck and Bomb. Red becomes even more agitated when his feathered brethren welcome green pigs to their island paradise. As the swine begin to get under his skin, Red joins forces with Chuck and Bomb to investigate the real reason behind their mysterious arrival.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The Walshes' Latin America road trip continues - they are now in Costa Rica, where Brad's fear of heights is tested as Barney's arranged a breathtaking way to take in the coastal views in what can only be described as a tiny roofless helicopter. With Costa Rica known for its diverse wildlife, the pair stop off to meet a quirky animal lover and then head to the capital city of San Jose, where they get their skates on as they join a local roller derby team for a match against their biggest rivals.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney's time in Costa Rica continues - inspired by childhood memories, the pair head to the country's cloud forest to attempt tree climbing, but this one is slightly taller than your average at 132 feet tall...

programme.desc: Claire won't let Phil buy a video game console; Jay and Gloria get into a disagreement over the types of toys Joe plays with; Haley wants to stand up for herself at work; Claire attempts to bribe the school principal.

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.desc: Jake and Charles join forces with the lead investigator at the U.S. Postal Service to catch a drug dealer; the team helps Amy quit smoking.

programme.desc: When Chief Wiggum uses a military jet pack to fight crime, he ends up crashing it into the church, leaving Marge and the congregation to resort to gambling to raise money for the repairs.

programme.sub-title: The Ziff Who Came to Dinner

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: 27-year-old eccentric big kid Chris is planning to make a bold statement with his wedding ideas for sensible bride-to-be, Corrine. programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride

Unfortunately Holly's plans were called off, so now Anthony has just three weeks and £12,000 to give Holly a wedding that's truly out of this world.

programme.title: Hear My Song
programme.desc: Mickey O'Neill wants the famous Josef Locke to perform at his nightclub. O'Neill's reputation was ruined when he hired an impostor claiming to be Locke and only a performance by the real Locke can make up for it.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Reap the Wild Wind
programme.desc: Loxi Claiborne owns a salvage company that operates off the coast of Florida. When a hurricane sinks the Jubilee, she races to claim salvage rights but is beaten by a competitor, King Cutler.

programme.title: Send Me No Flowers
programme.desc: George Kimball mistakenly believes he only has a few months to live, and arouses the suspicions of his wife Judy when he tries to pair her off with Bert Power as a potential future husband.

programme.title: The Last Wagon
programme.desc: Todd is an orphaned son of white settlers bought up by the Comanche. When his wife and children are killed by three brothers, he exacts appropriate revenge. Caught and charged with murder, he's sent by wagon train to face justice, but when the train is attacked by Apaches, he is the only male adult survivor and has to escort a rag-tag band of survivors to safety while the law set off in pursuit of their quarry.

programme.title: Decision at Sundown
programme.desc: A gunman is obsessed with tracking down and killing the man who stole his tramp wife. However, a gun duel reveals the truth.

programme.title: Taxi
programme.desc: A bumbling policeman enlists the aid of a skilful taxi driver to chase Brazilian bank robbers through New York.

programme.title: Nope
programme.desc: A man and his sister discover something sinister in the skies above their California horse ranch, while the owner of a nearby theme park tries to profit from the mysterious, otherworldly phenomenon.

programme.title: Monolith
programme.desc: While trying to salvage her career, a disgraced journalist begins investigating a strange conspiracy theory. However, as the trail leads uncomfortably close to home, she's left to grapple with the lies at the heart of her own story.

programme.title: Significant Other
programme.desc: A young couple take a remote backpacking trip through the Pacific Northwest and face sinister events.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James Martin's British adventure travels as far to the east of England as this journey will go, to Norfolk, where he is joined by his good old mate and top local chef, Galton Blackiston. They meet in Cromer, where Galton has a successful fish and chip shop, which inspires James to cook pan-fried cod with a warm tartar sauce on the beach. Then James heads to Norfolk Lavender to find out why it grows so well in this county and how it's distilled for oil.

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: Mary opens her garden to host a village fete and shares her brilliant flair for turning easy dishes into spectacular crowd pleasers. She's visited by two passionate brothers for a lesson in the in the secrets of authentic Italian pizza making.

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: This is indulgence at its best with Mary's mouth-watering dishes that give the everyday a lift with lashings of naughtiness. Treats are in store as Mary pursues the history of the Great British pie and tempts us with the secret to an easy sticky toffee pud.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree makes beef noodle salad bowls with peanut sauce, kale pasta Mason jar salad and a cowboy bento box packed with chicken wings in BBQ sauce and creamy lemon basil potato salad.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree is driving to town, but dinner's all ready to go when she gets home! On the menu is a hearty shepherd's pie, roasted carrots with vinaigrette, and for dessert, a stunning raspberry cream pie.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: On the menu is smoked salmon, Yorkshire pudding, beets and asparagus, plus gorgeous Greek chicken, sweet pepper and pea couscous with tzatziki.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: This time Jamie cooks glazed sizzling chops, sweet tomato and asparagus lasagnetti, plus mushroom soup, stilton, apple and walnut croutes.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Ina Garten welcomes old and new friends into her home for good food, great conversation and lots of fun. Ina prepares delicious treats for her guests, and they share their amazing life stories, along with some of their own signature dishes.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Ina Garten welcomes old and new friends into her home for good food, great conversation and lots of fun. Ina prepares delicious treats for her guests, and they share their amazing life stories, along with some of their own signature dishes.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining bakers take on more challenges set by judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith in the nation's most famous tent, in a bid to be crowned star baker.

programme.title: Rachel Allen: Bake!
programme.desc: Today's treats include Tarte Tatin, with caramelised apples. In Callandar, baker Tom Lewis reveals the secrets of his delicious bread.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James and Stephen head south through Wales en route to Pembrokeshire. But before they get there, they stop to visit Gareth Ward and his stunning restaurant Ynyshir. After filling their bellies, they make it to the coast where James cooks up his Marloes Sands seafood parcel. Stephen tracks down Wales' answer to Willy Wonka, young chocolatier Liam Burgess, who helps him make a very special one-off-chocolate bar for James.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James' British adventure has crossed over the Irish sea to Northern Ireland, where he is joined by his great pal and famous local chef, Paul Rankin. Their first port of call is the Giant's Causeway, where James settles right in and cooks up a fish stew for the crowds of tourists. Paul goes in search of Irish gold and finds it in the form of rapeseed at the Kane family farm in Limavady.

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: Mary opens her garden to host a village fete and shares her brilliant flair for turning easy dishes into spectacular crowd pleasers. She's visited by two passionate brothers for a lesson in the in the secrets of authentic Italian pizza making.

programme.desc: This time Jamie cooks glazed sizzling chops, sweet tomato and asparagus lasagnetti, plus mushroom soup, stilton, apple and walnut croutes.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: The Emmy-winning actor, comedian, writer and producer Julia Louis-Dreyfus joins Ina Garten at the barn to enjoy country French omelettes and a Beatty's chocolate cake frosting and piping masterclass.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Stephen Colbert and Evie McGee-Colbert visit Ina Garten for sour cream coffee cake and to chat about working in live TV. The Colberts whip up two recipes from their new cookbook: green goddess dip and Capri sunset cocktails.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining bakers take on more challenges set by judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith in the nation's most famous tent, in a bid to be crowned star baker.

programme.title: Rachel Allen: Bake!
programme.desc: Today's treats include Tarte Tatin, with caramelised apples. In Callandar, baker Tom Lewis reveals the secrets of his delicious bread.

programme.title: River Cottage: Road Trip
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall's journey begins in Dumfriesshire, where he learns how to make haggis.

programme.title: Forever Summer With Nigella
programme.desc: Nigella makes Corsican omelettes with goat cheese, a green salad with fresh mint and grilled halloumi. Next she prepares courgette fritters and a parsley and almond salad.

programme.title: Forever Summer With Nigella
programme.desc: Nigella takes inspiration from the southern and eastern Mediterranean, with saffron-scented chicken pilaf, semifreddo, Moroccan roast lamb and Turkish cacik - a refreshingly cool cucumber and yoghurt soup.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy samples surf and turf at a fish shack in Florida that serves fresh shrimp and a surf town cafe in California with a huge menu.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a farmer's market in South Carolina, an Italian spot in New York, and finds a fresh spin on a fish classic in Honolulu.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. In Los Angeles Guy visits an El Salvadoran place for tortillas, has a Basque bourguignon in Nevada, and has tapas in Florida.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. In California Guy tries a Tuscan-style Italian restaurant, in North Carolina he has fish tacos and in Honolulu it's shepherd's pie.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In St Louis, Adam attempts to down almost a gallon of the best milkshake in town.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: How will Adam fare when he faces a spicy ramen made with ten different chilies?

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy flaunts his cake - and dance - moves when he designs a flamenco cake. Meanwhile, Mauro and the team make a cake for a nearby summer camp, and it gets messy!

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: The team go back to school with a cake to mark an event for the Mayor of Newark providing school supplies for kids, and Madeline is tasked with a rainbow cake for a charity run!

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy rolls out for medium-rare done right.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Sauteed rib-eye with french fries; beef heart; fresh bread; schnitzel.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy finds a farm restaurant in New York, a fishing shop turned eatery in California, and a Mexican joint with tuna tacos in Ohio.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree shares the good stuff with the chocolate lovers in her life, starting with a triple chocolate tiramisu for her best friend. Then, with her son's help, she makes crispy chocolate dippers to use in a crazy chocolate shake.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond has money-saving ideas for transforming random cuts of beef into four delicious dishes, including slow cooker Sunday stew, teriyaki beef stir-fry, meat pies and grilled beef salad.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree makes four of her favourite recipes lighter and healthier in 16 minutes. Try her lighter chicken parmesan, Asian noodle salad, taco skillet and lighter fried pork chops with white beans and spinach.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree spends time with her son, Bryce, making beef fajita nachos. The next day, she prepares slow cooker white chicken chili for dinner and orange-vanilla monkey bread as a breakfast treat, while Bryce cooks meat and potato scramble.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond welcomes a new cowboy to the ranch with a frontier-style dinner kit of his favourite food, including short rib sandwiches, roasted butternut squash salad and salted caramel frosted brownies.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: It's comfort from start to finish and Ree's spelling it out in an old-fashioned, feel-good way. She cooks up tuna noodle casserole, meatballs in sweet and sour sauce, glazed apple dumplings and Welsh rarebit.

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Prue welcomes award-winning chef Tommy Banks as she makes two-tone soups and is joined in the kitchen by her husband John for a goat curd and tomato galette.

programme.title: Jamie's Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a frugal but amazing paella with chicken thighs, spicy chorizo and prawns from the freezer. There's also crisp Cajun fishcakes with a classic chopped salad and Jamie's snake in the hole.

programme.title: Jamie's Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie makes a thrifty curry with chicken drumsticks and frugal but posh beans on toast.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by Galton Blackiston, his pal and a top-class, Michelin-starred chef. They don diving suits to swim with seals, and hire an unusual mode of transport to take them around the islands. Galton visits a sea salt farm and delivers some back to James, who uses it to cook up some wonderful salt-baked sea bass. James finds an unusual restaurant situated in the middle of a post office, and finishes off the day cooking roast duck by a stunning seashore.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James returns to one of his favourite places in the UK: the beautiful county of Cornwall. Joining him is his close pal and Michelin-starred chef Galton Blackiston. During their trip they discover some world-class Cornish ice cream, and Galton visits Newlyn fish market to pick up some Cornish cod. James cooks the cod along with Cornish oysters and a warm tartare sauce. They both round off the day with a painting challenge on Sennen beach, where they try out their oil painting skills.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Rachel Allen uses her kitchen cunning to make delicious meals. Here, she uses local ingredients including Dublin Bay prawns and Irish potatoes.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Rachel and family re-invent a timeless classic, Roast Chicken and a wonderful Summery salad.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Simply delicious food for hot sunny weather is the order of the day as Rachel whips up a Nutty Quinoa Salad, a Potato, Smoked Salmon & Dill Tart and a Spinach, Bacon and Gruyère frittata.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: A slow recipe using lamb.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Kids: Food that the children will love and that Mum and Dad will most likely tuck into as well! Sun-Blushed Tomato or Parma Cam, Goats Cheese & Red Onion Pizza, Cheesy Chorizo Dough balls and Cookies & Cream Cheesecake are all rustled up in Rachel's kitchen, plus there are tracks of the trade from the cookery school and a glimpse of family life as Rachel and her daughter pick their own veg.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Rachel has some fantastic food ideas for home workers, including roasted butternut squash salad, and red lentil soup with cumin and coriander.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Irish chef Rachel explores the joy of cooking with home grown ingredients, with dishes including duck plum spring rolls with sesame pak choi.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Sample a typical working day for Rachel and discover foods which provide fuel for busy times, including smoked salmon and lemon risotto with asparagus.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Rachel makes some favourites designed to get everyone round the table, including cheesy meatballs with spaghetti, and butterscotch apple pudding.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Rachel's busy working life is again the inspiration, as she explores alternative takes on a packed lunch, and food that works on the table or on the go.

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Prue welcomes award-winning chef Tommy Banks as she makes two-tone soups and is joined in the kitchen by her husband John for a goat curd and tomato galette.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's going for amped-up pork tacos in Sacramento, CA, and a BBQ joint in Palm Beach Gardens, FL, is bringing brisket to a stacked sloppy joe. Plus, a real-deal Cambodian spot in San Jose, CA, is serving primo pork belly noodles.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.sub-title: From Bunny Chow to Chicken Oysters
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's digging into the unexpected! A South African spot in Black Mountain, NC, is cooking lights-out lamb curry, a chef in San Jose, CA, is bringing big flavour to beef barbacoa, and a Boynton Beach, FL, joint is serving up chicken oysters.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. In Tennessee Guy visits a spot that smokes the whole hog, a sausage cafe in Kentucky, and he finds stuffed pork chops in California.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. In California Guy visits a diner for meatloaf, a chilli spot in Cincinnati, and an Irish pub in Florida for a Guinness beef stew.

programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a farmer's market in South Carolina, an Italian spot in New York, and finds a fresh spin on a fish classic in Honolulu.

programme.desc: Patrick chairs two hours of spirited debate and news making interviews. Top guests tackle the issues that matter, with a sharp take on every story.

programme.title: Patrick Christys Tonight Late Edition
programme.desc: Patrick Christys explore tomorrow's news, tonight, including all the major stories making the front pages and full coverage of all the breaking news at home and abroad.

programme.title: Breakfast with Stephen and Ellie
programme.desc: Start your day with all the news and views from Britain's best Breakfast show.

programme.title: Britain's Newsroom
programme.desc: When the news happens, it happens here. But what does it mean for you? Every weekday morning, Britain's Newsroom brings you the biggest stories and the answers you need from the across the UK.

programme.title: Good Afternoon Britain
programme.desc: Every weekday from midday, we bring you the news as it breaks. Wherever it's happening, we'll have it covered - as well as giving our own view and how it matters to you with Tom and Emily.

programme.title: Martin Daubney
programme.desc: Martin gives an up-to-the-minute take on the day's events delivered in his own unique and lively style.

programme.title: Dewbs & Co
programme.desc: Plain-speaking, straight-talking - Michelle Dewberry delivers her individual take on the day's news.

programme.title: Lee Anderson's Real World
programme.desc: Lee Anderson brings the Real World to the Great British public, as the silent majority finally have their voices heard in the Conservative deputy chairman's brand-new current affairs show.

programme.title: Friday Night with Matt Goodwin
programme.desc: Britain was once the envy of the world. Join Matt Goodwin as he explores how we fix the problems facing the country.

programme.title: Patrick Christys Tonight
programme.desc: Patrick chairs two hours of spirited debate and news making interviews. Top guests tackle the issues that matter, with a sharp take on every story.

programme.desc: Patrick Christys explore tomorrow's news, tonight, including all the major stories making the front pages and full coverage of all the breaking news at home and abroad.

programme.desc: Patrick chairs two hours of spirited debate and news making interviews. Top guests tackle the issues that matter, with a sharp take on every story.

programme.title: Billy Graham Classic Crusades
programme.desc: World renowned evangelist, Billy Graham, who's ministry spans more than 60 years, shares timeless wisdom from his expansive television archive of life-changing sermons.

programme.title: Through the Bible - Andrew Wommack
programme.desc: Andrew Wommack presents this new show moving through scripture step by step, based on his Bible Commentaries garnered from his years of Ministry.

programme.title: Derek Prince
programme.desc: Known as a Modern Day apostle, the late Derek Prince's legacy lives on.

programme.title: David Jeremiah
programme.desc: Dr. David Jeremiah helps others discover turning points in their relationship with Jesus Christ.

programme.title: Creflo Dollar
programme.desc: For Dr Creflo A Dollar, excellence is a way of life.

programme.title: Believers' Walk of Faith
programme.desc: Learn how to live in victory with the Pastor of the Living Word Christian Centre, Bill Winston.

programme.title: The Gospel Truth With Andrew Wommack
Enjoying Everyday Life
programme.desc: Joyce Meyer shares life-changing messages from the Gospel.

programme.title: Joseph Prince
programme.desc: Pastor Joseph Prince focus on the superabundant grace of God and the perfect sacrifice of Jesus.

programme.title: Desire of All Nations
programme.desc: Nathan Morris of Shake The Nations is a British evangelist who has been on GOD TV since the Bay of the Holy Spirit Revival. He is known for his passionate messages on the Blood of Jesus and the Cross.

programme.title: Oxford Bible Church
programme.desc: Led by Derek and Hilary Walker, OBC aims to bring love, hope and healing to the world by teaching God's Word in the power of the Holy Spirit. Derek is an Oxford graduate, author and End-Time expert.

programme.title: Jonathan Bernis: Jewish Voice
programme.desc: Explore the Jewish Roots of your Christian Faith, and learn how Bible prophecy and today's world events surrounding Israel are connected to your life.

programme.title: A Significant Life -- Ps. Jim Graff
Rick Renner
programme.desc: Watch Rick Renner's in-depth, engaging Bible teaching, using the Greek text to help listeners gain new insight from God's Word.

programme.title: What's the Word with Bryan Wright
programme.desc: Bryan Wright of Boomerang Church, NC, approaches the Word with hunger and humility. This series carries on from his What's The Word Podcast.

programme.title: Experience him with tracy harris
programme.desc: Bringing the best in television programming.

programme.title: World Impact With Billy Wilson
programme.desc: The president of Oral Roberts University, Dr Billy Wilson, is a global influencer with strong ethics who is passionate about building up impactful leaders.

programme.title: David Hathaway
programme.desc: David Hathaway has been preaching the Gospel throughout Russia, Europe and Israel for over 70 years! He has seen the Power of God from the beginning of his ministry, and wants to keep the Fire going.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the chefs' last chance to impress and tensions are high. Dan takes a risk with an unusual combination of ewe's milk and bergamot while Danny is determined to pull out all the stops with his complex dessert `Aunty Gwen's Chocolate'. David hopes to transform a childhood comfort classic into a show stopping dessert. Dan also takes a trip to meet Dr Ben Clarke who was working in the Royal London Hospital A&E during the London Bridge terror attack.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again, but will they take on board their veteran's advice as they serve their `Feast To Say Thank You'?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Banquet champion Selin Kiazim, a rising star of the London scene renowned for her Turkish Cypriot fusion flavours, is back hoping to join the elite group of chefs who have made it to the banquet twice. She is up against James Cochran who's worked at some of London's toughest kitchens including 2 Michelin starred `The Ledbury' and Scott Goss from `The Twenty Six' in Tunbridge Wells, renowned for his use of local Kent ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs do battle for the fish course and the atmosphere in the kitchen is tense. Lobster is catch of the day for James whose dish is Caribbean-inspired. Scott is attempting to elevate a classic fish pie, dedicated to his mum who worked as a pharmacist in the NHS for 23 years. And Selin is taking a risk with a complex three-part fish dish featuring sardines, scallops and langoustines, as a nod to the purported health benefits of a Mediterranean diet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scott is showcasing rare breed Tamworth pork to say thank you to the rare breed of people who work for the NHS. Selin is serving a complex duck dish using ingredients from all around the world to honour `Our Global NHS'. And James is presenting five cuts of goat cooked in five different ways, for his bold dish `Under The Knife'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Selin puts her own unique twist on tea and toast. James creates a decadent coconut parfait accompanied by mango puree filled doughnuts for his dessert `Tree of Life' inspired by the coconut palm tree, the NHS and both of their contributions to our health. Scott hopes his elevated gypsy tart `Honey Bee Good' will secure him a place in the final.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and the South East chefs must cook their four-course menus again, but will they take on board their veteran's advice as they re-make their `Feast To Say Thank You'?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Vying to be crowned champion of Scotland are three talented newcomers to the GBM kitchen. Lorna McNee is a former Scottish Chef of the Year and is renowned for her technical precision which she has gained from working her entire career at 2 Michelin-starred restaurant Andrew Fairlie at Gleneagles.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the fish course and the chefs are all out to impress. The heat is increased as all three serve their unique takes on lobster. Ross is hoping to snatch victory with his dish `Lobster Aid'. Lorna's `Live Well, Live Very Well' features only three elements, each of which she needs to deliver perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Ross is hoping to impress with `Oh Deer It's Time For Your Penicillin', his tribute to Scottish scientist Alexander Fleming featuring an unusual yeast element as well as venison. Across the kitchen, Lorna, a former Game Chef of the Year, is also cooking venison in a bid to impress.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's dessert day and the last chance for the chefs to impress before one is sent home. Ross is attempting to elevate a comfort classic into a dish worthy of the banquet. Lorna's cooking a personal tribute to the NHS staff who have cared for her during treatment for a rare blood condition, using unusual yuzu and tonka bean flavours. Once again Ben is serving an experimental dish with his `Dance for the Greater Good' which includes a bees wax ice cream.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's judging day and the two remaining chefs must impress the panel of Oliver Peyton, Andi Oliver and Matthew Fort. Both chefs are gunning for success and are doing everything they can to deliver their menus to perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs compete in the heat for the central region. Returner Ryan Simson-Trotman, who failed to reach the national finals last year, is originally from Nuneaton and opened his own restaurant Orwells in the Oxfordshire countryside with his partner Liam, who is also a contestant in the North West region.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the fish course and the chefs are pulling out all the stops. Marianne pays tribute to the `angels' in her local hospital who looked after her during a recent illness. Ryan's dish is a reference to the purported health benefits of fish high in omega 3 oils. While Sabrina is inspired by her aunt, a retired nurse who used to take in food to share with her fellow workers during her shifts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their mains and following yesterday's results, tension is high. Both Marianne and Ryan are cooking muntjac in the hope of impressing the veteran judge, while Sabrina is making an ambitious technical take on a family feast.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With one dish left to impress the veteran judge, the chefs cook their desserts. Marianne is looking to raise a smile with her `carry on' themed dessert, complete with surprise presentation. Sabrina hopes to make it through with an elevated take on jelly and ice cream while Ryan goes all out with his very sweet treat `A Spoonful of Sugar'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining central region chefs do battle for a place in the national finals. They must impress the formidable panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton with their four course menus.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andrew is attempting to pay tribute to NHS staff with his innovative take on a traditional healing recipe, `Mum's Flu Fighting Chicken Soup'. Jason's `A Tea From The Heart' is a play on a post-war remedy which harks back to the era when the NHS was formed. And Chris is hoping to impress with his dish inspired by a therapeutic hospital garden featuring foraged herbs from the Welsh valley where he lives including wild chervil, pickled pine buds and yarrow tips.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After the starter results, the chefs return to the kitchen hoping for high scores with their fish dishes. Chris once again is using ingredients he has foraged from around his restaurant, while Jason hopes to impress with a spicy scallop dish and Andrew prepares a cod dish with 11 different elements.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are pulling out all the stops in a bid for big scores with their mains. All three dishes pay tribute to the founder of the NHS Aneurin Bevan, Chris's `Everything But The Squeal' is an ambitious pork sharing platter, while Jason's `For The Rich And The Poor' showcases lamb, and Andrew is hoping to impress with `Aneurin Bevan For Better Steak'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the dessert course and the last opportunity for the chefs to impress. Chris is hoping to triumph with another dish which showcases the foraged ingredients available around his restaurant, while Jason attempts a technical feat in his dessert inspired by a childhood hospital stay and Andrew is inspired by a dish by his great grandmother used to cook. Which two will make it through to the judging chamber?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the Wales regional final as the two remaining chefs do everything they can to impress the judging panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. After tensions ran high in the week, the chefs are under pressure to deliver perfection. The judges are joined by Aneira Thomas, the first baby born on the NHS, who is named in tribute to the service's founder Aneurin Bevan and who went on to dedicate her life to the service. Who will win a place in the national finals?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the turn of three chefs representing the South West, including two who have competed before but meet in the kitchen this week for the very first time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the fish course and with Tom renowned as a fish specialist all eyes are on him. He's cooking his take on a classic British fish supper in tribute to a charity which looks after the health of local fishermen. Jude is attempting a complex fusion dish featuring four different fish and an unusual X Ray themed presentation, while Olivia is paying tribute to the staff who looked after her during the birth of her first son with her take on curried monkfish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the main course and two of the chefs are hoping duck will win the day. Tom is preparing a technically challenging duck dish steeped in NHS history while Jude is making a multi-element Chinese duck dish inspired by his mum. Olivia is hoping her dish, using two unusual cuts of beef, will score top points. Olivia also visits her local children's hospital, home of the second busiest paediatric A&E in the country.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With just one more chance to impress their veteran judge, the chefs are all hoping to use their pastry skills to deliver technically complex desserts. Tom's cooking an `Invalid Fruit Tart' inspired by a 1950s recipe, while Jude is making six different apple elements for his `An Apple A Day'. Olivia is using stout in reference to an outdated old wives' tale which suggested the drink was a healthy source of iron during pregnancy.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's time for the two remaining chefs representing the South West to go head to head as they cook their menus again for the exacting panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. With a spot in the National Finals at stake, both are aiming for perfection as they bid for glory. The judges are joined by Barbara Childs, a Matron in the Cardiac Intensive Care Unit at Great Ormond Street Hospital, who isn't afraid to speak her mind in the chamber.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs take to the kitchen in a bid to represent the North West in the national final. They include Ellis Barrie, from the award winning Marram Grass in Anglesey, Liam Simpson-Trotman, an accomplished pastry chef and co-owner of the 4 AA rosette Orwell's in Oxfordshire, and Craig Sherrington, who sharpened his skills working under top names including Eric Chavot and is now Chef Patron of Virginia House in Cumbria.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the fish course and the chefs are all gunning for tens. Craig pays tribute to a friend who has revolutionised hospital food in Morecambe Bay, while Liam is inspired by health advice his grandma gave him and Ellis is dedicating his dish to the staff who delivered his son Albert. With cod, mackerel, shrimp and cockles on the menu, which of the chefs will score highly?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Time for the chefs to cook their mains and the pressure is on. Craig is hoping to impress using two cuts of Dexter beef, while Liam prepares his take on a Bajan lamb stew and Ellis prepares four cuts of suckling pig. Liam meets his personal hero, Nola Ishmael OBE, who became the UK's first ever black Director of Nursing.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the dessert course and the last chance for the North West chefs to impress in a bid to make it through to the regional final. Liam is cooking `Not So Hot Toddy' in honour of a friend who works nightshifts in the NHS, while Ellis' `Worth Their Weight in Gold' is dedicated to the staff who saved his brother's life and Craig's dish `The Missed Celebration' pays tribute to his wife with an unusual Christmas concept.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest scoring chefs from the week return to cook their menus again. Judging them are Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and Andi Oliver who are joined by Dr Jake Dunning MBE, an infectious disease specialist who has been recognised for his work during the West Africa Ebola epidemic.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs are doing battle to represent Northern Ireland. Tommy Heaney, Head Chef at the Great House Hotel in South Wales is taken on by Shauna Froydenlund, Chef Patron at Marcus Waring's two Michelin starred restaurant and James Devine, the 2017 National Chef of the Year.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After a tense starter course, the chefs return to the kitchen to cook their fish dishes. James is making his elevated take on a fried chicken takeaway as a tribute to the NHS staff who care for his sister. Shauna is also taking inspiration from a personal connection with a tribute to staff from a facility in her hometown of Londonderry. Tommy is hoping to impress with a dish dedicated to the discovery of DNA.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With two courses down it's time for the chefs to cook their main courses. It's the battle of beef with each using the same meat in their innovative creations. Tommy also meets the first baby ever born on the NHS.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's dessert course and the final chance for the chefs to impress. After such a tightly fought battle all week, each is hoping to deliver a technically perfect dessert. Tommy and James are both using passion fruit in their complex dishes while Shauna is hoping to use her pastry chef training to score full marks.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs representing Northern Ireland go head to head. Judging as ever are Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver who are joined by Shehan Hettiaratchy, lead trauma surgeon at St Mary's Hospital who helped treat victims of the Grenfell Tower fire.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The winning chefs from all 8 regions must now battle it out for the honour of a spot on 2018's `Feast To Say Thank You' banquet menu, celebrating 70 years of the NHS, one of Britain's most prized institutions.Each day the regional champions will be cooking one of their courses again for the discerning panel of judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs must cook their fish courses but after some exceptionally high scoring dishes in the starter round, everyone is desperate to deliver perfection in order to see off the competition. Once again the chefs present their innovative, witty or heart warming creations, with lobster, mackerel, John Dory, turbot and hake among the dishes being scrutinized.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's main course day and with only two spots remaining on the banquet menu, the competition is fierce. Both chefs and judges alike are amazed at the high standard of cooking and an incredible number of 10s are dished out as the chefs serve up venison, rabbit, duck, beef, lamb and pork for scrutiny.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's dessert day and with just one place remaining at the banquet feast, each chef is giving everything they've got for a chance to say thank you to the heroic NHS staff. All the chefs have significantly re-worked or re-invented their desserts since the regional heats, proving that they'll do everything they can in a bid for that final winning spot.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's dessert day and with just one place remaining at the banquet feast, each chef is giving everything they've got for a chance to say thank you to the heroic NHS staff. All the chefs have significantly re-worked or re-invented their desserts since the regional heats, proving that they'll do everything they can in a bid for that final winning spot.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: In the run-up to September 1939, political and economic chaos in the aftermath of World War I allowed Hitler in Germany, Mussolini in Italy and ultra-nationalists in imperial Japan to seize absolute power.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Germany took just four weeks to conquer Poland, using a new form of warfare - Blitzkrieg. Six armoured tank units known as Panzer divisions, supported by the Luftwaffe, quickly broke through Polish defences.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: After the retreat from Dunkirk, Churchill rallied Britain in its darkest hour. Hitler was only just across the channel, but he reckoned without our supreme strategist, the might of the navy and the manoeuvrability of the air force.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Explores Hitler's assault on the Soviet Union in the summer of 1941, Operation Barbarossa and his attack on the Caucasus in 1942.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: The attack on Pearl Harbour brought the 'sleeping giant', America, into the war, and saw the emergence of the aircraft carrier as the pre-eminent weapon of the seas.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Mussolini wanted to recreate the Roman Empire, but proved to be a poor military strategist and his incompetence contributed to Hitler's eventual defeat.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: With their armies fully engaged on the battlegrounds, the Allied and Axis powers focused their efforts to develop new weapons and new strategies to deliver a knockout blow to the enemy and bring about a quick end to the conflict.

programme.title: Myths & Monsters

programme.title: Myths & Monsters

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Built by William the Conqueror, the Tower of London is one of Britain's most iconic landmarks. It has been a military fortress, a palace, a royal mint, a prison, a zoo and a place of execution, a silent witness to some of the most momentous events in our history. Dan Jones reveals some of its fascinating secrets and tells the stories of the traitors, rebels and royalty who met their end inside its imposing walls.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan is in the heart of the English countryside at Warwick Castle. While it appears idyllic today, for centuries it was a poisoned chalice to the Earls of Warwick who lived there and its walls echo with tales of betrayal, bloodshed and murder.

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the story of Stewart Britain by visiting Stirling, the home of the Scottish royals who eventually took the English throne in 1603 and created Great Britain.

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Norwich, the UK's most Tudor city, Professor Alice Roberts learns about religious intolerance, uncovers the Norwich FC canary's origins and experiences humiliating Tudor justice.

programme.title: JFK: The Final Evidence
programme.desc: Explore the mystery behind the assassination of President John F Kennedy with Dr David J Hall.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: The team is in the north of Britain where they uncover evidence of the first Roman siege in Britain, Scottish man-made islands and a famous monastery.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Finds include: revelations from `Britain's Pompeii', the 3,000-year-old perfectly preserved village in Cambridgeshire, including how Bronze Age ancestors designed their homes, and their kitchens packed with food and equipment, and the theatre where Shakespeare premiered Romeo and Juliet and Henry V.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan is in the heart of the English countryside at Warwick Castle. While it appears idyllic today, for centuries it was a poisoned chalice to the Earls of Warwick who lived there and its walls echo with tales of betrayal, bloodshed and murder.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Historian Dan Jones is in the heart of Snowdonia exploring a stronghold built not only as an impenetrable fortress but also as a lavish royal palace fit for a fairytale - Caernarfon Castle.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Does Everyone Hate the British Empire?
programme.desc: Al and comedian Loyiso Gola embark on a road trip through South Africa to explore the UK's imperial history in the country, from colonial wars to the deals that eventually led to the development of apartheid.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Does Everyone Hate the British Empire?
programme.desc: Al and comic Kevin Kropinyeri reveal that Captain James Cook was not really a captain, explore the Aboriginal history of Australian cricket, and uncover the often brutal land grabs that impacted Indigenous Australians.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson is out to discover why Pembrokeshire feels so English. There are castles everywhere and it boasts one of the country's finest cathedrals, sure signs that the Normans stamped their mark all over this little slice of Wales.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony's walk takes him back to 1940 when the sleepy county of Dorset became the frontline in the war against Hitler. His five-day, 60-mile walk along the Jurassic coast reveals the county's hidden World War II story. Starting by the defences on Chesil Beach, Tony's journey encompasses stunning scenery and amazing acts of ingenuity and bravery as he heads east to finish in Swanage and Studland Bay.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores 2019's most exciting archaeological finds.

programme.desc: In broad daylight on a busy Woolwich street on 22nd May 2013, serving soldier Fusilier Lee Rigby was attacked and murdered by Islamic extremists Michael Adebolajo and Michael Adebowale.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: Beverley Allitt was thought to be a 'model' nurse. Very astute, caring and always willing to help, discover why she murdered four children and attempt to kill 11 others.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: A look at the murder of 18-year-old Sally Anne Bowman, who was stabbed in Croydon in 2005. With interviews with Bowman's family and detectives who solved the case.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: The wife of Garry Newlove, who was beaten to death outside his home in Cheshire in 2007, looks back at the night of her husband's murder.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: Eight year old Sarah Payne's disappearance and murder shocked the nation. Here, Sarah's mum reveals her tireless campaign to protect other children from paedophilia.

programme.desc: Police officers and witnesses reflect on David Copeland's nail bomb terror in 1999, when blasts in Soho, Brixton and Brick Lane killed three and injured over one hundred.

programme.desc: When a young mother is found dead in her car on New Year's Day, there's very little evidence. Almost 20 years later, her sister joins a rookie cop to catch the killer.

programme.title: Blood Runs Cold
programme.desc: A 45-year-old murder investigation gets fresh momentum when the victim's nephew takes the case on. And his search for the truth unearths hidden secrets.
